articles.headline: Two killed as fire guts Noida Sec 66 building
articles.summary: Two people, identified as Sneha (24) and Rishabh Kumar (27), died in a major fire at a four-storey apartment building in Noida's Sector 66. The blaze likely started from an electric scooter in the parking area. Police have arrested building owner Krishna Kumar and accountant Shreejan Das Gupta for negligence. The building reportedly lacked mandatory fire safety systems.

articles.headline: Adani denies quid pro quo on dropping of US indictment
articles.summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani submitted a sworn affidavit in a US court affirming he was not aware of any quid pro quo offer regarding the Department of Justice's decision to drop bribery and fraud charges against him. Adani noted his intent to invest $10 billion in the US was independent of legal developments, while the DoJ clarified that investment promises did not influence its decision.

articles.headline: Education helps women plug gender gap in Indian pay
articles.summary: Analysis of Periodic Labour Force Survey data shows the gender pay gap in India narrows as education levels increase. Salaried women earn about 76% of their male counterparts' average salary. While women school teachers now outnumber men, they remain a minority in higher-paying government schools and senior academic roles like professors, where the gender ratio falls to 0.5.

articles.headline: US strikes Iranian bases after naval blockade threats
articles.summary: The US military launched massive airstrikes against Iranian coastal defenses and missile sites on Wednesday. The action followed Tehran's threat to shut down the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's defense ministry stated the country is now in an 'existential war' with America. The strikes reportedly destroyed dozen of targets, including launch sites in Mianwala Chowk and Henjam Island.

articles.headline: Sule demands state-wise seat hike details for delimitation
articles.summary: NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule stated her party would support the proposed delimitation bill only if the government clarifies that every state will see a 50% hike in Lok Sabha seats. The statement comes amid concerns that southern states might lose relative representation. The new delimitation exercise is intended to be based on the first census conducted after 2026.

articles.headline: India, EU to enhance collaboration in critical technologies
articles.summary: India and the European Union have agreed to deepen cooperation in quantum technologies, 5G/6G, and semiconductors during a Trade and Technology Council (TTC) meeting. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and EU officials discussed setting standards for AI and securing global supply chains. The move aims to foster high-tech innovation and reduce strategic dependencies.

articles.headline: Illegal immigration from India to US drops 69%
articles.summary: US border authorities recorded a 69% decline in illegal encounters with Indian nationals, dropping from 67,000 in FY2023 to 20,614 currently. The sharp fall is attributed to tougher US immigration policies and increased enforcement at the southern border. While encounters at the US-Mexico border plummeted, those along the northern border with Canada saw a slight increase.

articles.headline: India loses 56 hours of sleep annually to heat
articles.summary: A new study reveals that people in India lose an average of 56 hours of sleep annually due to climate change-induced heat. Chennai recorded the highest loss at 93 hours per year, followed by Mumbai (84) and Kolkata (80). Experts warn that rising nighttime temperatures are disproportionately affecting older adults and low-income households without access to cooling solutions.

articles.headline: Farmers face income loss due to patchy monsoon
articles.summary: Nearly 53% of India's districts have received deficient or largely deficient rainfall so far this monsoon. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reports that essential crops like pulses, oilseeds, and maize are at risk due to long dry spells and high temperatures. Economists warn that reduced yields could lead to 12-17% higher food prices in the coming months.

articles.headline: Revanth Reddy starts ₹60k-cr Amazon data centre project
articles.summary: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy laid the foundation stone for a ₹60,000-crore Amazon Web Services (AWS) data centre at Bharat Future City. The project is expected to act as a catalyst for attracting global investments to the region. The CM emphasized his government's goal to make Telangana a $1 trillion economy by 2035 and a $3 trillion economy by 2047.

articles.headline: Kerala and TN to launch joint border drug patrols
articles.summary: The governments of Kerala and Tamil Nadu have agreed to conduct joint border patrols to curb drug trafficking. Named 'Operation Toofan', the initiative involves synchronized checking at state borders to prevent the flow of narcotics. Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan and TN officials finalized the plan following a surge in cross-border drug-related crimes.

articles.headline: Kerala HC seeks opinion on affordable cancer drugs
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court has sought expert opinions on whether a cheaper version of a patented breast cancer drug can be manufactured under compulsory licensing. The drug, Ribociclib, currently costs up to ₹27,000 per pack, making it inaccessible to many. The court is exploring Section 92 of the Patents Act to allow domestic production for public health reasons.

articles.headline: Nod for ₹18k-cr infra projects in three states
articles.summary: The government has approved road and railway infrastructure projects worth over ₹18,300 crore in Varanasi (UP), Odisha, and Jharkhand. Key projects include a six-lane elevated corridor in Varanasi and a 74-km fourth railway line in Jharkhand. The initiatives are designed to improve freight movement, reduce travel times, and enhance regional connectivity under the PM Gati Shakti plan.

articles.headline: PM Modi to flag off India's first hydrogen train
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to flag off India's first hydrogen-powered train during his upcoming visit to Haryana and Punjab. The train, which emits only water vapor, will run on the Jind-Sonipat route. This initiative marks a major step in the Indian Railways' goal to achieve zero carbon emissions and adopt green energy solutions for public transport.

articles.headline: FBI adds gangster Nitish Kaushal to most wanted list
articles.summary: The FBI has added Indian-origin gangster Nitish Kaushal to its list of most wanted fugitives. Kaushal is allegedly involved in an international racketeering conspiracy, money laundering, and drug trafficking in the US. He is believed to be linked to organized crime groups in Punjab. The US District Court has issued a warrant for his arrest, and the FBI is offering a reward for information.

articles.headline: Punjab likely to amend sacrilege law after clergy pressure
articles.summary: The Punjab government is planning to amend the state's sacrilege law following pressure from Sikh clergy and the SGPC. The proposed changes aim to make punishment for desecration of religious texts more stringent, including potential life imprisonment. A special assembly session may be convened soon to discuss the legal framework and address objections from religious bodies.

articles.headline: BRICS nations pledge support for women in the workforce
articles.summary: BRICS labor and employment ministers adopted a joint declaration to expand social security and boost women's participation in the workforce. The plan includes skill development for the digital economy and support for gig platform workers. The ministers also backed the development of an international classification of occupations for digital and green jobs to standardize global labor markets.

articles.headline: Indian sailor missing after Oman ship attack found dead
articles.summary: An Indian sailor, Herambh Karmarkar (30), who went missing after a Houthi attack on the cargo vessel MV GFS Galaxy off the coast of Oman, has been confirmed dead. The Indian Consulate in Dubai is coordinating with Omani authorities to repatriate his remains to Pune. The attack occurred on Sunday, and Karmarkar was one of 11 Indian crew members on the ship.

articles.headline: SC approves India's first night safari in Lucknow
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has granted permission for the establishment of India's first night safari at the Kukrail Reserve Forest in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh. The project had been pending environmental clearances and zoo authority approvals. The court allowed the state to proceed provided it complies with periodic inspections by the Central Zoo Authority to ensure wildlife safety and habitat preservation.

articles.headline: 679,000 Indian infants missed basic vaccines in 2025
articles.summary: A WHO-UNICEF report states that approximately 679,000 infants in India did not receive a single dose of essential vaccines in 2025. While 90% of children completed the full three-dose series of DTP, the number of 'zero-dose' children remains a concern. India is among the top countries with high numbers of unvaccinated infants, highlighting gaps in the national immunization program.

articles.headline: Cabinet clears ₹1.27 lakh cr Semicon 2.0 plan
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) with a budget of ₹1.27 lakh crore. The expanded mission aims to build a complete ecosystem, including essential chemicals and gases for chip manufacturing. The government expects the scheme to attract ₹4 lakh crore in investments and create over 5 lakh jobs, including 1 lakh direct roles in the sector.

articles.headline: India's unemployment rate remains steady at 5.5%
articles.summary: According to the National Statistical Office (NSO), India's headline unemployment rate stood at 5.5% in June, unchanged from May. The urban unemployment rate was recorded at 6.6%. While the overall rate was stable, data showed a marginal increase in women's labor force participation, which rose to 25.2%. The report highlights steady job growth in the manufacturing and services sectors.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹1.27 trillion for India Semiconductor Mission Phase 2
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has cleared the second phase of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) with an investment of ₹1.27 trillion. The phase focuses on silicon fabrication, display units, and compound semiconductors. Incentives include 40% support for silicon fabs and 35% for other units. The government aims to build a complete electronics value chain and support domestic design startups.

articles.headline: Small SIPs see rapid growth but face high closure rates
articles.summary: Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) with small amounts, such as ₹250, reached 3.22 lakh accounts by June 2026. However, SEBI data shows a high mortality rate for these accounts, with 26,000 closures in May 2026 alone. Market volatility and geopolitical tensions are cited as primary reasons why first-time small investors exit their investment plans prematurely.

articles.headline: Tata Motors urges government to review proposed CAFE-2 fuel efficiency norms
articles.summary: India's largest electric vehicle maker, Tata Motors, has requested the Ministry of Power and BEE to review changes to Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFE) rules. The company argues that a proposed credit-debit system could make paying penalties cheaper than investing in clean technology, potentially stalling the adoption of cleaner vehicle engines.

articles.headline: Indian vehicle sales hit record highs in first quarter of FY27
articles.summary: Passenger vehicle sales in India grew 25.9% to 12.7 lakh units in the April-June quarter. According to SIAM, commercial vehicle sales rose 18.3%, while three-wheeler sales jumped 29.7% to 2.14 lakh units. The growth was driven by increased activity in mining and cement sectors, urban demand, and easier availability of credit despite global economic uncertainties.

articles.headline: Tata Group to invest ₹10,000 crore in Kerala shipbuilding project
articles.summary: The Tata Group plans to enter the shipbuilding sector with a ₹10,000 crore investment in Kerala. CM V.D. Satheesan confirmed the proposal, stating the government will provide necessary land. This move aligns with India's goal to become a top five global shipbuilder by 2047. Currently, India holds less than 1% share in the global commercial shipbuilding market.

articles.headline: HDFC AMC reports 12% rise in Q1 net profit to ₹837 crore
articles.summary: HDFC Asset Management Company announced a 12% year-on-year increase in net profit for the June quarter, reaching ₹837 crore. Revenue from operations grew 14% to ₹1,100 crore. The company managed average assets under management (AAUM) of ₹9.4 lakh crore during the period, representing roughly 11.2% of the total mutual fund industry assets.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court hears challenge to CCI settlement framework validity
articles.summary: A petition in the Delhi High Court has challenged the constitutional validity of the settlement framework under the Competition Act. The plea argues that current rules prevent consumers and informants from questioning settlements reached between the CCI and companies under investigation for anti-competitive behavior, specifically targeting sections 48A and 48B of the 2023 amendment.

articles.headline: AI to drive efficiency and growth opportunities says Rishad Premji
articles.summary: At Wipro's 80th Annual General Meeting, Chairman Rishad Premji stated that AI will transform business processes and improve efficiency. He cited an example where AI could reduce financial closing cycles from 24 hours to just 8 hours. While acknowledging pressure on traditional IT revenue, he emphasized that AI integration would open new avenues for innovation and data modernization.

articles.headline: Union Bank of India Q1 profit grows 30% annually
articles.summary: Union Bank of India reported a strong performance for the first quarter of FY27, with net profit rising 30% on a year-on-year basis. The bank's stock closed at ₹172.4, up 1.1% following the results announcement.

articles.headline: Billionbrains Garage Ventures reports 94% surge in Q1 net profit
articles.summary: Corporate earnings for Billionbrains Garage Ventures showed a massive 94% year-on-year increase in net profit for the first quarter of FY27. The company's stock price reacted positively, ending 6.2% higher at ₹216.4.

articles.headline: Romeo Real Estate signs joint development deal for Mumbai project
articles.summary: Romeo Real Estate has entered into a joint development agreement for a large-scale residential project in Mumbai. The company's stock rose 1.3% to close at ₹713.2 following the disclosure of the joint venture.

articles.headline: IFSCA panel suggests Mortgage REITs to boost Gift City funding
articles.summary: An expert committee of the International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) has recommended the introduction of Mortgage REITs (mREITs) in Gift City. The panel also suggested dual listing of REITs and InvITs on international exchanges to provide domestic real estate projects with better access to global capital and diversified investment options.

articles.headline: New IPO listings deliver average gains of 26% since March
articles.summary: Despite market volatility caused by US-Iran tensions and FPI selling, 16 out of 23 companies listed since March 2026 are trading above their issue prices. Investors have earned an average return of 26% on these listings. Omnitech Engineering emerged as the top performer with a 147.5% gain, while small and mid-cap stocks generally outperformed the broader indices.

articles.headline: Mutual Funds increase equity stake via block deals and QIPs
articles.summary: Mutual funds used block deals, Qualified Institutional Placements (QIPs), and stake sales to increase their equity holdings in June. Major investments were seen in Adani Enterprises, JSW Infrastructure, and Acme Solar Holdings. Total net equity investment from mutual funds reached ₹32,000 crore for the month, offsetting some institutional selling in the secondary market.

articles.headline: SEBI tightens code of conduct for board members and officials
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI has overhauled its administrative framework by introducing a strict 17-page code of conduct for board members. The new rules mandate comprehensive disclosure of financial assets, liabilities, and professional interests. Members must now freeze or sell conflicting investments before taking office to prevent conflicts of interest and ensure transparency.

articles.headline: US expected to ease proposed Section 301 tariffs on Indian goods
articles.summary: The US is likely to soften its stance on proposed 12.5% additional tariffs on Indian goods under Section 301. The dispute involves allegations of forced labor in supply chains. India recently issued a notification defining forced labor in accordance with international standards, which is expected to address US concerns and provide relief to Indian exporters.

articles.headline: RBI issues draft framework for data maintenance by banks and NBFCs
articles.summary: The RBI has proposed a new framework requiring banks and NBFCs to establish dedicated data maintenance committees. Boards must oversee data risks, quality, and classification to ensure accuracy and security. The guidelines, effective from April 2027, aim to strengthen the technical infrastructure of financial institutions as they adopt Expected Credit Loss (ECL) models.

articles.headline: India initiates first $140 million export shipment to UK under TEPA
articles.summary: Under the new Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA), India has dispatched its first export consignment worth $140 million to the UK. The shipment includes automobiles and agricultural products. As part of the deal, India will immediately reduce tariffs on Scotch whiskey from 150% to 75%, eventually reaching 40% over ten years, while the UK offers duty-free access for Indian textiles.

articles.headline: NHAI to execute two major road projects near Varanasi for ₹25,445 crore
articles.summary: The government has approved road expansion projects for NH-31 and the Varanasi Ring Road with a total outlay of ₹25,445 crore. The projects, spanning 43.22 km, will be developed under the Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM). These link roads will feature flyovers and elevated corridors to boost tourism and facilitate the transport of minerals like coal and limestone in the region.

articles.headline: EPFO reports 48.7% of accounts have final balances below ₹20,000
articles.summary: Data from the EPFO reveals that nearly half of its members held between ₹10,000 and ₹20,000 at the time of final settlement. To prevent the exhaustion of retirement funds, new rules mandate a minimum balance must remain in accounts after partial withdrawals for illnesses or education. EPFO expects this to increase long-term savings for nearly half its member base by up to 35 times.

articles.headline: Female unemployment in India rises to 15-month high of 5.9%
articles.summary: The latest Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) shows India's female unemployment rate rose to 5.9% in June, up from 5.6% in May. While rural female unemployment stands at 5%, the rate in urban areas is significantly higher at 8.4%. The overall unemployment rate for the month was recorded at 5.5%, while the Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) remained stable at 54.4%.

articles.headline: Index of Service Production shows broad growth in April 2026
articles.summary: The Ministry of Statistics released the testing Index of Service Production (ISP), showing that 14 out of 19 sub-sectors recorded double-digit growth compared to the previous year. Housing and food services led with 37.2% growth, followed by retail trade at 30.8%. However, the air transport sector witnessed a sharp 14% decline in activity during the same period.

articles.headline: Editorial: India must focus on skilling and human capital for growth
articles.summary: The editorial argues that India's journey to becoming a high-income nation is hindered by systemic failures in human capital development. It emphasizes that physical infrastructure investment must be matched by spending on basic education and health. To avoid the middle-income trap, the government must effectively transition labor from low-productivity agriculture to high-skill service roles.

articles.headline: Astronaut Sunita Williams and Russian team reach ISS via Soyuz
articles.summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully docked at the International Space Station (ISS) after a three-hour journey. Indian-origin astronaut Sunita Williams and two Russian cosmonauts joined the existing crew. They are scheduled to stay for eight months to conduct scientific research and test new space technologies, with a planned return to Earth in April next year.

articles.headline: Former ISRO Chairman S. Somanath joins Agnikul Cosmos as director
articles.summary: Private space startup Agnikul Cosmos has appointed former ISRO chief S. Somanath to its board of directors as an observer. The appointment comes as the company prepares for its Mission 02, which aims to launch the Agnibaan rocket. The mission will focus on developing reusable rocket boosters and orbital platforms, marking a significant step for India's private space sector.

articles.headline: NDA seeks opposition support for 131st Constitution Amendment Bill
articles.summary: The NDA government is reaching out to opposition parties, including NCP(SP) and DMK, to build consensus for the 131st Constitution Amendment Bill ahead of the Monsoon Session. The bill aims to reserve one-third of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies. The opposition remains cautious, demanding a clear formula regarding the proposed 50% increase in total parliamentary seats.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves ₹1.9 Trillion Plan for Chips and Mobile Phones
articles.summary: The Union cabinet approved a new manufacturing strategy featuring the ₹1.27 trillion Semicon 2.0 program to boost domestic chip design and R&D. It also cleared a ₹62,500 crore mobile phone incentive scheme to increase local value addition to 45%. The policy aims to build indigenous brands and technology patents, targeting ₹39 trillion in mobile production by FY31.

articles.headline: Indian Refiners Use UAE Ports to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: To avoid conflict risks in West Asia, Indian oil refiners are shifting energy supply routes to UAE's Fujairah and Khor Fakkan ports. Importers are also using the 406km Habshan-Fujairah pipeline to skip the Strait of Hormuz. Previously, 60% of India's crude and 90% of LPG imports passed through the strait, which is currently a focal point of US-Iran maritime tensions.

articles.headline: RBI Questions Banks Over Slower Foreign Currency Deposit Inflows
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has asked commercial banks to explain the slow response to its foreign-currency deposit scheme, which has raised only $7 billion since June. Despite RBI absorbing hedging costs, the scheme trails expectations of $30-40 billion. Banks cited high overseas funding costs and investor uncertainty regarding rupee depreciation as major hurdles.

articles.headline: Indian Investors Pour Record Funds into Global Equity Markets
articles.summary: Assets under management for India-domiciled global equity funds reached ₹98,182 crore in May 2026, a six-fold increase since 2020. The surge is driven by interest in US tech stocks and AI themes. Experts warn of a potential bubble as Nifty 50 performance lags behind the Nasdaq, urging caution regarding geographical diversification and currency risks.

articles.headline: India Launches Trial Index of Services Production to Track GDP
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has launched a trial Index of Services Production (ISP) to monitor activity in the services sector, which accounts for 53% of India's economy. The index uses GST data and administrative records to track 19 sub-sectors. April data showed 14 sectors recorded double-digit growth, led by retail trade and telecommunications, while air transport saw a decline.

articles.headline: China Risks Missing 5% Growth Target as June Quarter Slows
articles.summary: China's GDP grew by 4.3% in the June quarter, falling short of official projections and hitting its lowest goal since 1991. The slowdown is attributed to weak domestic consumption, oversupply, and reaching saturation in export markets. Despite the slump, China continues significant tech advancements, including the successful test of its Long March 10B reusable rocket.

articles.headline: India Remains Lower-Middle-Income Nation While Asian Peers Rise
articles.summary: India missed the upgrade to upper-middle-income status in 2026 as Vietnam, the Philippines, and Sri Lanka moved up the ladder. India's per capita GNI stands at $2,760, roughly half the threshold for the next category. While India remains the fastest-growing major economy, rapid aggregate growth is not yet translating into higher individual incomes as quickly as its peers.

articles.headline: Bodhi Tree in Talks for Majority Stake in Adyar Ananda Bhavan
articles.summary: Bodhi Tree Systems, backed by James Murdoch and Uday Shankar, is in advanced discussions to acquire a significant stake in restaurant chain Adyar Ananda Bhavan. The deal values the company at ₹3,000-3,500 crore. The promoters seek fresh capital to expand the 140-outlet chain globally. The deal may involve a special purpose vehicle and structured debt from Bain Capital.

articles.headline: Low-Cost Indian AI Models Undercut OpenAI and DeepSeek Pricing
articles.summary: Indian AI firms BharatGen and Sarvam AI are offering foundation models at a fraction of the cost of global rivals. BharatGen’s Param-2 costs ₹5 per million tokens, compared to ₹191 for OpenAI’s cheapest class. The pricing advantage is partly due to government GPU subsidies, though experts note Indian models still trail global rivals in reasoning capabilities.

articles.headline: Larsen & Toubro Deploys Microsoft AI Copilot for Entire Workforce
articles.summary: L&T is rolling out Microsoft Copilot licenses to over 140,000 employees, nearly 80% of its workforce, to boost productivity. This makes it one of the first Indian conglomerates to deploy AI tools at such scale. The initiative aims to enhance decision-making and automate workflows across its engineering and IT services arms, though analysts warn of high future subscription costs.

articles.headline: SpiceJet Reports Severe Flight Delays Amid Financial Stress
articles.summary: One out of every seven SpiceJet flights was delayed by more than two hours in May, the worst performance among large Indian airlines. The carrier’s delay rate of 15.75% was 20 times higher than IndiGo's. The delays coincide with a shrinking fleet and a cash crunch, causing market share to drop from 3.9% to 3%. DGCA mandates meals and refreshments for such extended delays.

articles.headline: Auto Giants Target Booming Electric Scooter Fleet Market
articles.summary: The rapid growth of quick commerce and bike taxis is driving Bajaj Auto, Ola Electric, and VinFast to target the electric two-wheeler fleet segment. Vietnam's VinFast is exploring fleet deployments for an early India foothold. Bajaj has seen 36% revenue growth from its stake in Yulu, which deployed 48,000 units in FY26. Manufacturers are shifting focus to high-use gig workers.

articles.headline: India Reduces Unvaccinated Zero-Dose Children to 679,000
articles.summary: Latest WHO-Unicef estimates show India reduced its number of "zero-dose" children from 909,000 in 2024 to 679,000 in 2025. The country is no longer among the top 10 nations with the highest number of unvaccinated children against measles. The progress highlights successful immunization drives targeting infants who previously received no vaccines in their first year.

articles.headline: Urban Jobless Rate Rises to 6.6% in June
articles.summary: India’s overall unemployment rate for people aged 15 and above remained steady at 5.5% in June. However, urban unemployment rose to 6.6% from 6.4% in May, while rural unemployment eased slightly to 5.0% from 5.1%. The statistics ministry reported that the overall labor force participation rate remained unchanged at 54.4%, with urban participation showing a slight improvement.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry Orders Quarterly Management-Union Meetings at PSBs
articles.summary: The Union finance ministry has directed all 12 public sector banks to institutionalize quarterly meetings with recognized employee unions. The move aims to improve workplace culture and address issues like staff shortages and mental health before they escalate. Meetings must be attended by officers of at least General Manager rank. Major unions have raised concerns over the mandate.

articles.headline: Weather Department Forecasts Below-Normal Monsoon for July
articles.summary: The IMD has predicted subdued rainfall for west-central and south Peninsular India over the next week. July rainfall is forecasted at less than 94% of the long-period average. This follows the fifth-driest June since 1901. Heatwave warnings have been issued for Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu, raising concerns for kharif crop yields and food price inflation.

articles.headline: India Drafts First National Code for Climate-Resilient Breakwaters
articles.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards is developing the country's first indigenous code for the planning and design of breakwaters. The new standard requires structures to have a 50-year service life and factor in 100-year return-period storms and projected sea-level rises. This move reduces reliance on foreign manuals and aims to protect expanding maritime infrastructure from climate risks.

articles.headline: TVS Holdings Subsidiary to Acquire Varthana Finance for ₹967 Crore
articles.summary: Home Credit India Finance, a TVS Venu Group company, has executed an all-cash share purchase agreement to acquire 100% of Varthana Finance. The ₹967 crore transaction will expand TVS Holdings' presence in the financial services sector. Varthana, formerly known as Thirumeni Finance, focuses on school funding and educational loans, marking a strategic pivot for the group.

articles.headline: Wealthtech Emerges as India's Biggest Fintech Profit Driver
articles.summary: A Jefferies report identifies wealthtech as the primary earnings engine for India's fintech sector, contributing ₹7,400 crore in profit after tax for FY25. While payments lead in revenue share at ₹51,200 crore, that segment posted an aggregate loss of ₹5,300 crore. Total fintech industry profits are projected to rise from ₹2,300 crore now to ₹43,000 crore by FY30.

articles.headline: HealthKois Launches $300 Million Fund for Biotech Innovation
articles.summary: Venture capital firm HealthKois is targeting biotech and drug discovery for its $300-million third fund. The firm sees an 'inflection point' in Indian life sciences, with 1,095 drug discovery programs currently active across 195 companies. The fund plans to invest $7-25 million per company in startups focused on AI-led drug discovery, precision medicine, and vaccine platforms.

articles.headline: Container Corp Volumes Grow 9% Beating Street Estimates
articles.summary: Container Corp of India (Concor) reported 9% year-on-year volume growth in Q1FY27, reaching 1.4 million TEUs. Export-import cargo grew 10%, surpassing management guidance. Analysts expect further growth following the commissioning of the western dedicated freight corridor, which links Dadri to JNPT. The stock rose over 6% as business updates showed resilience despite West Asia trade issues.

articles.headline: Vibe Coding Platform Emergent Raises $130 Million Series C
articles.summary: Emergent has secured $130 million in funding co-led by Creaegis and MNI Ventures, valuing the company at $1.5 billion. The startup specializes in 'vibe coding'—AI-generated code created via conversational prompts. The capital will be used to expand its research team and automate processes for small businesses. Existing investors including SoftBank and Lightspeed also participated.

articles.headline: Zoho Launches AI-Powered Platform for Global Education Market
articles.summary: Zoho has introduced an AI-powered platform for schools and universities, offering features like automated lesson planning, AI tutoring, and grading assistance. The software will be free for government institutions and individual teachers with up to 100 students. Zoho is also developing an education-specific large language model (LLM) to support academic-specific use cases.

articles.headline: India-UK Trade Deal Halves Import Duties on Scotch Whisky
articles.summary: The India-UK FTA has immediately reduced import duties on Scotch whisky and gin from 150% to 75%, with a target of 40% over the next decade. Retail prices for imported spirits are expected to fall 7-10%. While beneficial for consumers, the cuts pose a challenge for India’s growing single malt industry. Bulk Scotch imports will also become cheaper for Indian premium blends.

articles.headline: Foreign Insurers Hike Stakes as India Reforms Recast Sector
articles.summary: Regulatory shifts toward risk-based capital and 100% ownership allowance are driving foreign insurers to increase Indian investments. Aviva and QBE have moved to 100% ownership in their JVs, while Prudential is acquiring a 75% stake in Bharti Life. Implementation of Indian Accounting Standards (Ind AS) and digital health claim exchanges are bringing the sector to global standards.

articles.headline: OpenAI Plans Movable Smart Speaker with Humanlike Personality
articles.summary: OpenAI is developing its first consumer hardware: a movable, screen-free smart speaker designed as a home AI companion. The device will feature mechanical elements that move to create a 'living' feel and will use an advanced version of ChatGPT Voice Mode. It aims to control smart homes and answer queries proactively, marking a move into direct competition with Apple and Amazon.

articles.headline: US Steps Up Strikes on Iran and Reimposes Blockade
articles.summary: The US has intensified airstrikes against Iranian military targets and reimposed a naval blockade in retaliation for attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Strikes hit an army barracks in Sistan and Baluchistan, killing at least seven troops. The escalation has shredded a recent interim ceasefire, threatening global energy supplies as oil prices rose 11% over two days.

articles.headline: UAE Gains Access to Advanced AI Chips After Supporting US
articles.summary: The US has granted the UAE expanded access to coveted AI chips, treating it like European allies for technology exports. The move rewards the UAE for its military support against Iran and efforts to keep oil moving through the Strait of Hormuz. The change allows Emirati firm G42 to buy Nvidia chips freely for nine months, despite concerns in Congress over potential technology diversion.

articles.headline: Apple Gets Chinese Approval to Roll Out iPhone AI Features
articles.summary: Apple received government approval from the Cyberspace Administration of China to launch its 'Apple Intelligence' AI features in the country. The iPhone maker was included on a list of approved providers alongside Huawei and Xiaomi.
