articles.summary: Nearly 120 Isro scientists have resigned in the past year, prompted by opportunities in the private space sector. In response, the Department of Space (DoS) has issued a memorandum tightening rules on voluntary retirement and resignations. It specifies that requests from key personnel in flagship programs like Gaganyaan and Chandrayaan-3 may not be accepted to ensure project continuity.

articles.headline: SC asks CBSE for details to curb digital evaluation flubs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the CBSE to detail steps taken to prevent errors in the Onscreen Marking (OSM) system. This follows a PIL highlighting irregularities that caused "look at the frustration caused to young children" due to incorrect evaluations. The court has appointed a one-member commission to investigate and invited the Solicitor General's assistance to resolve the issue.

articles.headline: HC rules booze whiff alone insufficient to prove drunk driving
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court ruled that the mere smell of alcohol on a driver's breath is not enough to sustain a charge of drunk driving. The court emphasized that scientific evidence, such as a breathalyzer test or blood alcohol test, is mandatory to establish intoxication levels before a person can be charged under relevant sections of the BNS for causing an accident.

articles.headline: NCP(SP) hints at potential support for government delimitation bill
articles.summary: Supriya Sule of the NCP(SP) suggested that her party might support the NDA's proposed delimitation bill if it offers a uniform 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats across all states. However, the decision will only be finalized after consulting with other Maha Vikas Aghadi (MVA) allies. The bill aims to increase parliamentary representation based on the latest census data.

articles.headline: Iran threatens to halt energy exports as US strikes continue
articles.summary: Iran has threatened to stop all regional energy exports following a series of US airstrikes on its military facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. The US military stated the strikes were in retaliation for recent attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The escalating tension has caused a surge in global oil prices and fears of a full-scale regional conflict.

articles.headline: Axar Patel enters top-10 in ICC ODI all-rounder rankings
articles.summary: Indian cricketer Axar Patel has entered the top-10 of the ICC ODI rankings for all-rounders. He jumped three positions to reach the ninth spot following his brilliant performance in the opening match against England in Birmingham. His consistent bowling and useful lower-order batting have made him a key asset for the Indian team in the 50-over format.

articles.headline: Karnataka HC rules kids have fundamental right to play in apartments
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that apartment owners' associations cannot prevent children from playing in designated play areas. Justice Hanchate Sanjeevkumar observed that playing is a fundamental right of children and essential for their development. The court directed an association to reconsider its decision to ban kids from playing, emphasizing that curbs must be reasonable.

articles.headline: Bannerghatta Road stretch beautified by ‘Parivarthan’ volunteer initiative
articles.summary: A 200-metre stretch of Bannerghatta Road near Dairy Circle has been transformed by a month-long cleanup and beautification drive called ‘Parivarthan’. Over 50 volunteers from Youth for Parivarthan cleared large quantities of mixed waste, painted murals, and repaired footpaths to make the area walkable. The stretch was previously a chronic black spot prone to public urination.

articles.headline: Ranga Shankara’s AHA! festival set to host international children's theatre
articles.summary: The 16th edition of the AHA! International Theatre for Children Fest will be held at Ranga Shankara from July 18-25. The festival features groups from South Korea, Austria, Switzerland, France, Germany, and India. The event aims to provide children with immersive, meaningful experiences that foster creativity and empathy through high-quality visual and physical storytelling.

articles.headline: Residents ask BDA to withdraw proposed PRR-NICE link road
articles.summary: Nearly 1,000 families in Banashankari Enclave and surrounding areas are opposing a proposed 3km link road connecting the Peripheral Ring Road (PRR-1) to NICE Road at Madavara. Residents claim the project will displace them from homes built with lifelong savings. They have submitted representations to the BDA, urging them to utilize existing alternate alignments instead.

articles.headline: Unemployed man arrested for killing grandmother in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A 24-year-old unemployed man, Prajwal, was arrested for allegedly murdering his 70-year-old grandmother, Gowramma, in Bengaluru. The accused was reportedly angry after his grandmother advised him to be more responsible and find a job. He allegedly slit her throat with a kitchen knife before fleeing the spot. Police tracked him down near Bannerghatta.

articles.headline: Electronics demand surges in engineering admissions; CS still top choice
articles.summary: In the first round of Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA) seat allotments, computer science and related branches remain the most popular choices. However, there is a significant surge in demand for electronics and communication engineering. Out of 1.4 lakh available seats across various courses, 91,724 have been allotted, with many top college cut-offs being highly competitive.

articles.headline: Influencer booked for harassing restobar staff in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A social media influencer known as 'Amaku Dumuku' was booked for allegedly harassing staff at a restobar in aluru. The influencer reportedly refused to pay his bill, made obscene remarks to a woman manager, and threatened consequences for publishing the incident. Police registered a case under sections related to sexual harassment and criminal intimidation.

articles.headline: Black magic racket busted in Kolar; seven arrested
articles.summary: Police in Kolar busted a black magic and faith-healing racket operating from a makeshift center at Aivarahalli village. Seven individuals were arrested, and six women were rescued. The group allegedly targeted vulnerable people by claiming to cure chronic illnesses through rituals. Police seized various items used in the rituals and are investigating further.

articles.headline: HC says life is beyond monetary valuation in drowning case
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court awarded ₹15 lakh compensation to the parents of a two-and-a-half-year-old boy who drowned in an open water tank at a construction site. The court rejected the ₹2 lakh settlement offered by the accused, stating that the loss of a child's life cannot be reduced to a figure that scarcely reflects the enormity of the tragedy.

articles.headline: Suicide attempt by Class 7 student; parents allege teacher harassment
articles.summary: A 13-year-old student is in critical condition after attempting suicide in Bengaluru. His parents alleged that a teacher at his private school humiliated and physically assaulted him for a fight with another student. The school management denied any caste-based bias, claiming the boy was only reprimanded. Police have registered a case under the Juvenile Justice Act.

articles.headline: Placement firm director booked for allegedly cheating job aspirants
articles.summary: A director of a placement firm in Marathahalli was booked for allegedly defrauding engineering graduates. The firm allegedly promised jobs in MNCs, collected fees ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹2,000 for fake recruitment exams, and then failed all candidates. Over 20 aspirants approached the police after realizing they had been duped. Investigations are ongoing.

articles.headline: 103-year-old woman wins 35-year legal battle for plot of land
articles.summary: In a remarkable case from Bihar, 103-year-old Habiban Khatoon won a legal battle for a small plot of land that lasted 35 years. The dispute began in 1993 when she was wrongfully dispossessed of her property. A civil court in Motihari finally ruled in her favor, restoring her title to the land and ending decades of litigation.

articles.headline: New technology helps EPFO clear 83% of claims faster
articles.summary: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has implemented an automated 'IT Enabled Services' system that has resulted in faster clearing of 83% of claims. The system allows for automatic processing of claims filed by subscribers without manual intervention. This tech shift has significantly reduced the backlog of pending interest and pension cases.

articles.headline: 9,911 more MBBS seats approved; total intake crosses 1.36 lakh
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has approved 9,911 new MBBS seats for the 2026-27 academic session, bringing the total national intake to over 1.36 lakh. Uttar Pradesh received the highest number of new seats, followed by Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu. The increase is aimed at meeting the growing demand for medical education and improving the doctor-patient ratio in India.

articles.headline: Tirupati temple receives record one-day donation of ₹97 crore
articles.summary: The Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD) recorded its highest-ever single-day collection of ₹97 crore from the hundi. This surge was due to a massive donation from a group of devotees. The TTD board chairman stated that these funds would be used for various charitable activities, including free meals for pilgrims and enhancing facilities at the temple complex.

articles.headline: Sonam Wangchuk firm on fast; CJP plans Parliament march
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, demanding constitutional safeguards for Ladakh. Concurrently, the Chhatra Yuva Sangharsh Samiti (CJP) has announced a march to Parliament to highlight issues related to youth unemployment and educational reforms. Protesters are seeking a meeting with the Union Home Minister to address their grievances.

articles.headline: Bombay HC affirms ‘right to be forgotten’ in quashed FIR cases
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court reaffirmed the 'right to be forgotten' as part of the fundamental right to privacy. The court ordered its registry to remove a petitioner's name from online records and search results related to two criminal cases that were quashed years ago. The ruling emphasizes that individuals should not be stigmatized by old legal proceedings that did not lead to convictions.

articles.headline: HC: Intimacy does not imply consent in rape by deception cases
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a prior consensual relationship does not mean subsequent instances of intimacy are consensual, especially in cases of 'rape by deception'. The court upheld a lower court's conviction of a man who allegedly used emotional manipulation and false promises of marriage to exploit a woman sexually.

articles.headline: Kolkata explores revival of tram network for sustainable urban transport
articles.summary: Kolkata is considering a comprehensive plan to revive its historic tram network as a clean urban transport solution. Experts argue that trams can serve as a secondary transit system to connect metro stations and provide last-mile connectivity. The proposal includes modernizing existing tracks and introducing electric trams to reduce pollution and traffic congestion.

articles.headline: Emergent becomes unicorn after raising $150 million in funding
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based startup Emergent has raised $150 million in a new funding round, valuing the company at $1.5 billion. The startup, which uses AI to help enterprises build and run their own applications, plans to use the capital for product development and global expansion. This makes Emergent one of the latest entrants into India's growing unicorn club.

articles.headline: British luxury cars to get cheaper under India-UK FTA
articles.summary: Under the newly signed India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA), import duties on British luxury cars like JLR and McLaren will be significantly reduced. This is expected to lead to a price drop of up to 15% for high-end models. However, tariffs on Scotch whisky remain unchanged for now, with further negotiations expected to address the liquor industry's demands.

articles.headline: Warren Buffett ends decades-long donations to Gates Foundation
articles.summary: Warren Buffett has announced he will stop his annual donations to the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation. Instead, he will direct his remaining fortune to a new charitable trust managed by his three children. Buffett had donated over $36 billion to the Gates Foundation since 2006. He cited a shift in his philanthropic strategy as the reason for the change.

articles.headline: Spain’s tactical mastery wins Euro 2024 final against France
articles.summary: Spain defeated France 2-0 in the Euro 2024 final to claim their fourth European championship title. Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro secured the victory. Spain's high-pressing game and tactical discipline were praised globally, while France's attack, led by Kylian Mbappe, failed to convert chances. Lamine Yamal was named the young player of the tournament.

articles.headline: Apartment owners get briefing on new law
articles.summary: Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar and Minister Krishna Byre Gowda interacted with over 500 apartment owners in Bengaluru about the draft Karnataka Apartment (Ownership and Management) Act, 2026, assuring protection of property rights.

articles.headline: Proposed law may not be perfect, but it is a good start: Krishna Byre Gowda
articles.summary: Greater Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda stated the proposed Karnataka Apartment (Ownership and Management) Bill, 2026, is a positive step despite not being a perfect solution, acknowledging complex issues.

articles.headline: Apartment residents seek builder accountability, parking norms in new Bill
articles.summary: Bengaluru apartment associations suggested amendments to the proposed Karnataka Apartment Bill, 2026, including builder accountability, association governance, parking management, dispute resolution, and internal renovations.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Heat Collective launched
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Heat Collective, initiated by 11 organizations, was launched to advance coordinated action on rising urban heat, focusing on risk mapping, health systems, cooling shelters, and worker protections.

articles.headline: Government considering splitting of Bengaluru Police Commissionerate
articles.summary: Karnataka government is considering splitting the Bengaluru City Police Commissionerate into two or three units, following the division of BBMP, to improve focus on expanding urban areas.

articles.headline: Homestay owner’s arrest illegal: HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court declared the arrest of a Kodagu homestay owner illegal after a U.S. citizen's alleged rape at his property, ordering the State to pay ₹5 lakh compensation for unlawful detention.

articles.headline: Four drug peddlers who challenged police nabbed
articles.summary: Hebbal police arrested four inter-State drug peddlers, including a Nigerian national who challenged Karnataka Police on social media, seizing 17.58 kg of MDMA crystal valued at ₹34 crore.

articles.headline: Six held in Tumakuru for drug peddling
articles.summary: Tumakuru police arrested six persons, including two students, for allegedly supplying ganja and hydroponic cannabis, seizing narcotics and a luxury car worth ₹44.3 lakh.

articles.headline: Panel backs re-incorporation of ‘Bauddha’ word in Naada Geete
articles.summary: A committee led by Purushothama Bilimale has recommended re-incorporating the word 'Bauddha' (Buddhism) into Karnataka's Naada Geete, citing its presence in Kuvempu's original draft.

articles.headline: SIR extended till Aug. 8; poll panel to organise special weekend camps
articles.summary: The Election Commission extended the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Karnataka until August 8 and will hold special weekend camps to assist voters in submitting forms.

articles.headline: ‘Fill and return enumeration forms even if 2002 electoral roll details are unavailable’
articles.summary: Karnataka CEO V. Anbu Kumar urged voters to submit enumeration forms even if 2002 electoral roll details are missing, as current exercise is for collection and digitisation, not correction of existing particulars.

articles.headline: BJP accuses government of issuing PRCs to Bangladeshi citizens
articles.summary: BJP complained to Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, alleging the Congress government is illegally issuing Permanent Resident Certificates (PRC) to Bangladeshi citizens to create a "vote bank," undermining federalism.

articles.headline: CET-2026: First round seat allotment results announced; admission deadline July 24
articles.summary: Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) announced first-round seat allotment results for professional courses, with 1,22,512 seats allotted. Candidates must complete admission by July 24.

articles.headline: Lokayukta pulls up Ramanagara officials over lake pollution
articles.summary: Karnataka Lokayukta directed Ramanagara Deputy Commissioner and Channapatna Tahsildar to take immediate action against dumping into a village lake and cremations on its bund, citing serious public health risks.

articles.headline: Karnataka ranks last among peer States in per capita urban grants: Report
articles.summary: A Janaagraha report reveals Karnataka ranks last among comparable States in per capita State Finance Commission grants to cities, receiving ₹2,244 compared to Kerala’s ₹6,251, citing stagnant allocation and lack of municipal elections.

articles.headline: Class 7 student attempts to end life after alleged classroom punishment
articles.summary: A 13-year-old Class 7 student in Bengaluru allegedly attempted suicide after being made to stand outside his classroom all day for mischief. Police registered an FIR against school administrators and teachers.

articles.headline: Woman detained for ‘stealing’ conductor’s bag on BMTC bus
articles.summary: A woman was detained in Bengaluru after allegedly stealing a BMTC bus conductor's duty bag, including cash, on a moving bus; a video of the incident went viral on social media.

articles.headline: We have lost trust in CM, say farmers
articles.summary: Farmers opposing the Bidadi Township Project expressed distrust in Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar despite his announcement of a review committee, citing broken promises and unresolved technical issues.

articles.headline: New committee to cool off rising political tensions?
articles.summary: Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar's decision to form a committee to re-examine the Bidadi Township Project is seen as a political balancing act to unite the Congress and defuse farmer agitation.

articles.headline: Another man dies of COVID in A.P.; 3 more test positive
articles.summary: In Andhra Pradesh, a 66-year-old man with comorbidities died after testing positive for COVID-19. Three new cases were reported: two in Mangalagiri and one in Visakhapatnam.

articles.headline: SIT submits final report in Dharmasthala case
articles.summary: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) submitted its final report to the Belthangady court regarding perjury and criminal offences alleged against six individuals in the Dharmasthala case, with further arguments set for August 29.

articles.headline: The crisis at the heart of non-proliferation
articles.summary: An opinion piece discusses the enduring hypocrisy and contradictions within the global non-proliferation order, highlighting the unequal treatment of nuclear states versus non-nuclear states like Iran.

articles.headline: Revelling in the power of visual politics
articles.summary: An opinion piece explores how Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay utilizes imagery and digital strategy, leveraging his cinematic popularity, to command public attention and drive his political agenda.

articles.headline: How challenging will Delhi’s ambitious EV transition be?
articles.summary: An analysis questions Delhi's aggressive EV transition targets, particularly for two-wheelers by 2028, citing the massive twelve-fold jump needed in EV sales and current slow adoption rates.

articles.headline: Kudankulam plant data leak sparks ‘commotion’
articles.summary: Over 19,000 files from KKNPP, including engineering blueprints and vendor lists, were accessed by World Leaks from a contractor's server, prompting investigations. NPCIL initially dismissed similar 2019 incident.

articles.headline: SC raps panel mandated to protect people from NE States
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticized the monitoring committee for northeasterners' problems, asking if it was merely "all talk" and questioning its effectiveness in addressing issues like racial discrimination and targeted violence.

articles.headline: ED files PMLA complaint in alleged visa fraud racket
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate filed a prosecution complaint against Red Leaf Immigration Private Limited and others under PMLA for an alleged organized immigration and visa fraud racket, based on U.S. Embassy complaints.

articles.headline: Congress asks Centre to clarify on U.S. Bill to curb social media content seeking 100% tariffs
articles.summary: Congress urged Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to clarify the Centre’s position on a U.S. Senate Bill proposing 100% tariffs on India and four other countries for buying Russian oil, calling it "humiliation."

articles.headline: SC refuses to entertain plea to curb social media content
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused a writ petition to curb podcasts and social media reels, stating Article 32 cannot be used to politicize issues, and advised filing a complaint for derogatory content against Prophet Muhammad.

articles.headline: C-section complications row: Kin of five Rajasthan women appeal to President for help
articles.summary: Families of five women at NMCH, Kota, wrote to President Murmu seeking euthanasia permission if kidney transplants aren't provided, suffering complications after C-sections. Rajasthan government ordered a probe.

articles.headline: Rath Yatra is Puri’s most spectacular festival: President
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu shared a personal account of her devotion to Lord Jagannath ahead of Puri’s Rath Yatra, highlighting the festival's message of equality and its significance.

articles.headline: Panel defers final meeting on higher education Bill
articles.summary: The Joint Committee of Parliament deferred its meeting to finalize the Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan Bill, 2025, to July 20, following objections from the Andhra Pradesh government regarding constitutional friction and state autonomy.

articles.headline: Wangchuk loses 8.9 kg as fast for Pradhan’s resignation continues
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike for Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation entered its 18th day, with his health deteriorating and civil society groups expressing concern.

articles.headline: Wife says activist’s decision to join CJP protest was organic
articles.summary: Sonam Wangchuk’s wife, Gitanjali J. Angmo, stated his decision to join the Cockroach Janta Party protest was "organic" and driven by his commitment to prevent the cause from being sabotaged, seeking constitutional safeguards for Ladakh.

articles.headline: Plea in Delhi HC seeks steps to save Sonam Wangchuk
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court sought responses from the Centre and Delhi government on a PIL seeking urgent intervention to safeguard the health of activist Sonam Wangchuk, whose health deteriorated during his fast at Jantar Mantar.

articles.headline: Mamata comes to Abhishek’s defence, says he is still fighting
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress chairperson Mamata Banerjee defended her nephew Abhishek Banerjee, stating he continues to "fight like a tiger" despite facing numerous cases and alleged Central agency targeting.

articles.headline: Faction led by Madan Mitra leaves Mamata, joins rebel Trinamool faction
articles.summary: Senior leader and MLA Madan Mitra joined the rebel Trinamool faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee, a day after the ED issued summons to him, marking another setback for Mamata Banerjee.

articles.headline: Marginal dip in number of rural job scheme workers
articles.summary: Following the transition from MGNREGS to VB-G RAM G, registered rural job scheme workers fell by 67.6 lakh (2.5%), and active workers by 26.3 lakh (2.43%), sparking concerns about digital compliance.

articles.headline: In meeting with House panel, Ladakh villagers seek access to grazing lands along the LAC
articles.summary: Ladakh villagers told a parliamentary panel they lost access to traditional grazing grounds along the LAC since 2020 due to border tensions with China and sought controlled access, highlighting local communities' security contribution.

articles.headline: Govt. taking nation towards economic crisis, says Congress
articles.summary: Congress accused the Modi government of steering India towards an economic crisis, citing May's 9.87% wholesale inflation (highest in 44 months) and rising food inflation, demanding an explanation from the PM.

articles.headline: Govt. issues public consultation guidelines for highway projects
articles.summary: The Ministry for Road Transport and Highways issued guidelines for mandatory public consultation in future highway projects' DPR stage, following Public Accounts Committee recommendations, to engage local officials and communities.

articles.headline: Adani denies any deal linked to dismissal of case in the U.S.
articles.summary: Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani denied under oath any promise or agreement related to the U.S. Department of Justice dismissing a criminal indictment against him, responding to a court order.

articles.headline: Indian Gas Exchange files IPO papers; parent to divest stake
articles.summary: Indian Gas Exchange Ltd. (IGX) filed preliminary papers for an IPO, which is an Offer For Sale of up to 1.67 crore equity shares by its promoter, Indian Energy Exchange Ltd (IEX).

articles.headline: Global bids invited under PLI for 10 GWh ACC batteries
articles.summary: The Ministry of Heavy Industries invited global bids to establish 10 GWh advanced battery manufacturing plants under the PLI scheme, aiming to reduce dependence on imported Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACCs).

articles.headline: India-U.K. FTA a boost for farmers and small,medium firms, says PM
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi stated the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), effective Wednesday, will boost Indian farmers, entrepreneurs, and MSMEs, opening new frontiers for services and mobility.

articles.headline: Indian government set to invest ₹200 cr. in Ather Energy
articles.summary: The Government of India will invest ₹200 crore in Ather Energy through the India-Japan Fund (IJF). Founders and Hero MotoCorp are also participating in the fundraising round.

articles.headline: RBI issues data governance guidance framework for banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued 'Guidance on Regulatory Expectations for Data Governance' for banks and Regulated Entities, strengthening data quality, accountability, risk management, and compliance with DPDP Act, 2023.

articles.headline: SEBI bans board from new stock, related instruments
articles.summary: SEBI updated its Code of Conduct Rules for Board Members 2026, classifying permitted and non-permitted investments for members and bringing Whole Time Members under insider trading rules.

articles.headline: India-U.K. social security pact is not retrospective
articles.summary: The India-U.K. Double Contributions Convention (DCC), effective Wednesday, exempts temporary workers from social security payments for 60 months but is not retrospective, applying only to employees arriving on or after July 15.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka is ‘always willing’ to host India-Pak. sporting events: Minister
articles.summary: Sri Lanka's Sports Minister Sunil Kumara Gamage stated the country is "always willing" to host sporting events between India and Pakistan, citing proximity and strong ties, and is also reforming cricket administration.

articles.headline: Spain’s masterclass dismantles France’s star-power
articles.summary: Spain defeated France 2-0 in the FIFA World Cup semifinal with goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro, reaching their first final since 2010 through relentless pressing, immaculate possession, and defensive organization.

articles.headline: Men in Blue look to build on the positives
articles.summary: India, after a six-wicket victory in the first ODI against England, aims to continue its positive performance in the second match, despite disappointing returns from Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli.

articles.headline: Good show by India in Asian age-group boxing
articles.summary: India secured five gold, 10 silver, and four bronze medals in the Asian U-19 & U-23 boxing championships in Jakarta. Chandrika Bhoresh Pujari (51kg) and Prachi (57kg) clinched gold in the U-19 women’s category.

articles.headline: Lord’s Test shows how well women cricketers can adapt to the demands
articles.summary: The first-ever Women’s Test at Lord’s was a resounding success, with India dominating England. The attendance set a new record, reflecting the evolution of the women's game and their adaptability to the longest format.

articles.headline: McIlroy seeks to end British Open drought
articles.summary: Rory McIlroy aims for his first British Open win since 2014 at Royal Birkdale, while World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler defends his Claret Jug, amidst McIlroy's recent Grand Slam successes.

articles.headline: BFC signs defender Sandro Embalo
articles.summary: Bengaluru FC signed defender Sandro Embalo from Indonesian club PSBS Biak for the 2026-27 Indian Super League season, strengthening their squad.

articles.headline: DYES ‘A’ posts win
articles.summary: DYES ‘A’ defeated KSPSPB 2-0 in the 10th Hockey Karnataka League Championship.

articles.headline: Arnav causes a flutter in the tennis world
articles.summary: 18-year-old Arnav Paparkar became the first Indian since Leander Paes (1990) to reach the Wimbledon quarterfinals, with a clinical 6-2, 6-1 win over Japan's Ryo Tabata.

articles.headline: High-quality field for Quantbox Chennai Grand Masters
articles.summary: The fourth edition of the Quantbox Chennai Grand Masters chess tournament begins, featuring reigning world champion D. Gukesh, Alireza Firouzja, and Nodirbek Abdusattorov.

articles.headline: Mixed results for jr. women’s hockey team in Europe
articles.summary: The Indian junior women’s hockey team had three wins and four losses during an exposure-cum-competition tour of the UK, preparing for the Under-21 Asia Cup.

articles.headline: Lakshya, Sagar in big stand as India makes a strong reply
articles.summary: Lakshya Raichandani's unbeaten 196 and Sagar Virk's 134 led India under-19 to 378 for three, replying to Sri Lanka's 424/9 decl. on day three of the Youth 'Test' in Galle.

articles.headline: Everyone wants to win gold at the Asian extravaganza
articles.summary: Sarvesh Kushare, after breaking Tejaswin Shankar's 8-year-old high jump National record with 2.31m, aims for gold at the Asian Games. His coach, Jithin Thomas, credits patience, focus, and a controlled run-up for his success.

articles.headline: The charge of the youth brigade in Season 7
articles.summary: Ultimate Table Tennis (UTT) Season 7 highlights young paddlers' energy and fearlessness, swinging momentum and challenging rankings with their skill, composure, and daring performances, such as Anna Hursey's unbeaten run.

articles.headline: U Mumba registers season’s first win
articles.summary: U Mumba TT secured its first win of Ultimate Table Tennis Season 7, defeating Dabang Delhi 8-7, with Anusha Kutumbale pulling off a crucial victory in the final singles match.

articles.headline: A new era of Indian ecology looks to its horizons, and to the ground
articles.summary: The second Indian Wildlife Ecology Conference (IWEC) in Sonepat explored how climate change, habitat fragmentation, and development reshape ecosystems, with researchers focusing on predicting ecological change and leveraging local institutions.

articles.headline: China’s ’Great Green Wall’ tames desert growth, but fight not over
articles.summary: China’s decades-long "Great Green Wall" program, using techniques like "straw checkerboards," has reduced desertified land by over 1,000 sq. km annually since 2000, but scientists warn continued effort is needed.

articles.headline: The problem with China’s 4.3% growth
articles.summary: China’s economy grew 4.3% in Q2 2026, its slowest since 2022 and below target, facing challenges from a real estate slump, sluggish consumption, and job market issues. Exports are a bright spot, raising questions for trade partners.

articles.headline: Digitisation and efficiency in criminal justice system
articles.summary: An analysis discusses India's plan for a full digital rollout of its criminal justice system by July 1, 2027, highlighting reforms in criminal laws (BNS, BNSS) aimed at a more democratic, citizen-oriented approach and improved procedural efficiency.

articles.headline: ICC Tweaks 2027 ODI World Cup 'Super 7', 2028 T20 World Cup Formats
articles.summary: The ICC approved format changes for the 2027 ODI World Cup (14 teams, 'Super 7' stage, replacing Super 6/quarter-finals) and the 2028 T20 World Cup (Super 10 instead of Super 8). The 'Super 7' stage will see top seven teams from Round 1 compete in a round-robin to decide semi-finalists. These tweaks aim to enhance consequences, competitive intensity, and competition across global events.

articles.headline: Spain Beats France 2-0, Reaches FIFA World Cup Final
articles.summary: Spain defeated France 2-0 in a one-sided FIFA World Cup semi-final to reach the final. Mikel Oyarzabal converted a penalty in the 22nd minute, and Pedro Porro doubled the lead in the 58th minute. French captain Kylian Mbappe criticized his team's 'sloppy' performance. Spain now awaits the winner between England or Argentina in the final.

articles.headline: US Senators Propose 100% Tariffs on Russian Oil Buyers, Including India
articles.summary: A bipartisan group of US senators introduced the 'Sanctioning Russia Act,' a bill proposing up to 100% tariffs on oil imports from Russia. It targets top five buyers like India, China, Hungary, Slovakia, and Azerbaijan, aiming to curb Moscow's energy revenues. India's Russian oil imports reached a record 25.8 lakh bpd in June (50% of total crude), making this bill a significant potential impact. The US President can waive sanctions for national interests.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves ₹1.9L Cr Schemes for Chips, Mobile Manufacturing
articles.summary: The Union cabinet approved two major schemes with a total outlay of ₹1.9 lakh crore: ₹1.27 lakh crore for semiconductors and ₹62,500 crore for mobile phone manufacturing. These schemes aim to bolster India's electronics ecosystem, reduce import dependence, and create 60,000 jobs. The mobile scheme seeks to raise local smartphone component share from 18% to 40% in five years, while the semiconductor push creates an indigenous manufacturing base.

articles.headline: SC Seeks Report on CBSE On-Screen Marking Glitches, Reforms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court asked the Union government and CBSE for a status report detailing 'remedial steps' to fix technical glitches and systemic failures in the CBSE's On-Screen Marking (OSM) system for Class 12 answer sheets. The court acknowledged student "frustration" due to evaluation discrepancies and scoring errors, directing CBSE to consider broader reforms for error-free digital assessment after multiple petitions.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves New Urea Policy for Self-Reliance, 1 Crore Ton Production
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved a new National Urea Investment Policy to establish 8-9 new gas-based manufacturing plants. Aiming for self-sufficiency and ending import dependence, the policy seeks to add 1 crore tons to domestic production, bridging the gap between 4 crore tons consumption and 3 crore tons production. Each new plant will produce 12.7 lakh metric tonnes annually, saving $30-50 million in foreign exchange per 10 lakh tons produced domestically.

articles.headline: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3% Amid Property Crisis
articles.summary: China's economic growth slowed to 4.3% in the second quarter, its slowest pace since late 2022 and below the government's annual target. The slowdown is attributed to a persistent property market crisis, weak domestic demand, low consumer confidence, deflationary pressures, and rising energy costs. Despite strong export growth and existing stimulus measures, structural headwinds persist, prompting calls for more aggressive government intervention.

articles.headline: RBI Approves Ex-CEC Rajiv Kumar as HDFC Bank Part-Time Chairman
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has approved former Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as HDFC Bank's part-time Chairman for a three-year term. A retired 1984-batch IAS officer, Kumar served as CEC until February 2025 and previously as financial services secretary. He succeeds Keki Mistry. His first board meeting on July 18 will review Q1 results and is ahead of the CEO's term review.

articles.headline: Sun Pharma Gets South Africa Nod for Generic Semaglutide Injection
articles.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries received regulatory approval from SAHPRA in South Africa to manufacture and market a generic version of the Semaglutide injection for Type 2 diabetes. The drug will be launched as a multi-dose pre-filled pen. South Africa is now the second market, after India, where Sun Pharma has secured approval for this specific generic treatment.

articles.headline: Kudankulam Nuclear Plant Data Leaked from Third-Party Server
articles.summary: The ransomware group 'WorldLeaks' posted over 19,000 sensitive files from the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant on the dark web, including blueprints and ventilation layouts for units 3 & 4. Reliance Infrastructure, a contractor, confirmed a "partial breach" on a third-party Yotta server. While NPCIL asserts core systems are unaffected, experts warn of critical security vulnerabilities. The incident sparked "commotion" and highlights critical infrastructure supply chain risks.

articles.headline: US Strikes Iran; Iran Threatens Persian Gulf Oil Blockade
articles.summary: US Central Command conducted airstrikes on Iranian missile and defense sites on Greater Tunb Island in the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to degrade Iranian military capabilities after Iran targeted commercial shipping. In response, Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatened to blockade all oil and gas exports from the Persian Gulf if US strikes continue or its own exports are restricted, causing Brent crude prices to near $85 per barrel.

articles.headline: Nationwide Public Health Drive Focuses on Preventative Care, Vaccinations
articles.summary: A nationwide public health awareness campaign has been launched, focusing on preventative care, promoting healthy lifestyles, and early disease detection. The initiative targets prevalent diseases with vaccination drives. Community health workers and centers are central to the outreach, aiming to improve national health outcomes for all.

articles.headline: Cultural Heritage Restoration Underway; Education Programs Launched
articles.summary: Efforts have begun to restore a significant cultural heritage site, preserving historical architecture and promoting tourism with international expert collaboration. Concurrently, educational programs are being introduced in schools, including field trips and workshops, to foster appreciation for national traditions and a sense of identity.

articles.headline: Major Political Party Unveils Election Strategy and Manifesto
articles.summary: A major political party has unveiled its comprehensive election strategy and launched its manifesto, detailing its vision and policy commitments across economic growth, social welfare, and national security. Focusing on key voter demographics and policy pledges, campaign rallies are set to begin next month across various states. Opinion polls are closely watched as public debates are expected to intensify.

articles.headline: National Sports, Policy Reviews Underway for Performance and Governance
articles.summary: A review of the national sports team's recent international performance is underway, focusing on athlete development for upcoming tournaments. Concurrently, a comprehensive review of national policies is assessing their impact on economic performance, social welfare, and environmental indicators. Policymakers are discussing the report's findings to refine strategies for inclusive growth and sustainable development, integrating diverse stakeholder feedback.

articles.headline: Renewable Energy Sector Sees Discovery, Policy Update, Capacity Expansion
articles.summary: Scientists announced a groundbreaking discovery in renewable energy, potentially boosting solar power conversion efficiency, requiring further R&D for commercial application. Concurrently, the government has outlined plans for significant renewable energy capacity expansion, especially in solar and wind power, with an updated policy introducing new subsidies and larger wind farm initiatives. This aims to reduce fossil fuel reliance, meet climate targets, and accelerate the clean energy transition, encouraging private investment.

articles.headline: International Relations Forum Discusses Global Challenges, Seeks Solutions
articles.summary: An international relations forum was convened, bringing together diplomats and policy experts to discuss pressing global challenges including geopolitical shifts, multilateral cooperation, global stability, trade, and climate change. Participants exchanged views on strategies for peace and stability, focusing on collaborative solutions for world issues. Joint resolutions were explored, and bilateral discussions on strategic partnerships also took place.

articles.headline: Nationwide Vocational Training Expanded, Includes Advanced Digital Skills
articles.summary: Vocational training centers are expanding nationwide, with programs updated to include advanced digital skills. These initiatives aim to equip youth and unemployed adults with practical skills for various industries, meeting market demands and improving employability for the modern economy. Partnerships with private and tech companies are crucial for industry relevance and success.

articles.headline: EC Extends Special Voter Revision in Four States Until August 8
articles.summary: The Election Commission extended the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls by 10-12 days until August 8 for Karnataka, Delhi, Telangana, and Punjab. This aims to help voters, especially in Karnataka where many couldn't trace 2002 roll details. Special weekend assistance camps will be held across Karnataka, including Bengaluru, Shivamogga, and Mysuru. This pushes back deadlines, with final rolls publishing on October 19.

articles.headline: Karnataka Releases Draft Apartment Bill for Unified Governance
articles.summary: After a decade, the Karnataka government has released a revised draft of the Karnataka Apartment Ownership and Management Bill, aimed at overhauling outdated laws and creating a unified legal framework. This bill seeks to streamline ownership, management, redevelopment, and dispute resolution for apartment complexes. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar has invited public suggestions until August 6 before it is tabled in the state legislature.

articles.headline: Cauvery Water Committee Defers Release Decision to July 28
articles.summary: The Cauvery Water Regulation Committee (CWRC) has deferred its decision on releasing water to Tamil Nadu until July 28. This comes as Karnataka argued for very low reservoir storage due to a weak monsoon and negligible inflows in June. The committee will review the situation, hoping for monsoon rains in the Cauvery catchment area, before issuing any water release directions to either Karnataka or Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: CM Announces Panel to Review Bidadi Township Project Amid Protests
articles.summary: Karnataka CM D.K. Shivakumar announced a committee to review the controversial Bidadi Township Project, first proposed in 2006, clarifying it's not his "dream project." Amid farmer protests and a data leak, the government issued final notifications for acquiring over 4,944 acres across four villages, totaling 5,462 acres across seven villages, for the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township. The CM assured no forcible land acquisition, offering compensation for voluntary submissions.

articles.headline: Mob Attacks Assam Rifles Camp in Manipur; Vehicles Torched
articles.summary: A mob attacked an Assam Rifles camp in Manipur's Senapati district, leading to clashes, property damage, and four torched vehicles. The incident was triggered by intelligence-led search operations near an NSCN (I-M) camp, which the local community found provocative, sparking tensions. Security forces are on high alert across the state to prevent further escalation of violence, with no casualties officially confirmed yet.

articles.headline: NDA Pushes for Two-Thirds Majority on Delimitation Bill
articles.summary: The NDA is intensifying efforts to secure a two-thirds majority in Parliament for the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, ahead of the Monsoon Session. The bill proposes raising Lok Sabha seats to 850 and initiating delimitation. This push involves outreach to INDIA bloc parties and has sparked realignment speculation in Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: BSF to Fence 90km of Sundarbans Along Bangladesh Border
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry is moving forward with plans to fence approximately 90 km of the challenging Sundarbans region along the India-Bangladesh border in West Bengal. The BSF is initiating technical feasibility studies and addressing local concerns regarding land acquisition for the project.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves Semicon 2.0, Mobile, Urea, Varanasi Highway Projects
articles.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved multiple schemes including ₹1.27 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, ₹62,500 crore for the mobile phone manufacturing scheme, and nine new gas-based urea plants with 10 MT capacity. Additionally, two Varanasi highway projects worth ₹25,400 crore also received Cabinet approval.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM, Leaders in Delhi for Cabinet Expansion Talks
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar, former CM Siddaramaiah, and KPCC president B.K. Hariprasad are in Delhi to discuss the much-awaited Cabinet expansion with the Congress high command. With 20 vacancies open, many MLAs are lobbying for berths.

articles.headline: Trump Threatens Iran Power Plants; Iran Warns of Oil Blockade
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to expand US strikes on Iran next week, targeting power plants and bridges if Tehran fails to make a deal. This comes as US forces continue strikes and reimpose a naval blockade, prompting Iran to warn of an expanded oil blockade if US actions persist.

articles.headline: Vultures CC Advances in KSCA U15 Girls', U19 Tournaments
articles.summary: Vultures Cricket Club advanced in two separate KSCA tournaments. They defeated Cambridge Cricket Club by nine wickets in the quarterfinals of the under-15 girls’ T30 tournament. Separately, Tejas Reddy's knock of 87 off 46 balls powered Vultures CC past Swastic Union Cricket Club (2) into the final of the under-19 inter-club tournament.
